wiki_catalogs.name: Project Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the Pico-DSP-Garden project overview section. Explain the framework's purpose as an embedded audio playground for RP2350/RP2040 microcontrollers, its dual-core architecture, and relationship to the broader Raspberry Pi Pico ecosystem. Document the core philosophy of 'Wire a DAC. Write a callback. Make sound.' and how it simplifies real-time audio programming. Include both conceptual overviews for beginners new to embedded audio and technical details for experienced developers. Use terminology consistent with the codebase including terms like 'producer-consumer pattern', 'I2S DMA streaming', and 'DaisySP integration'. Provide practical examples demonstrating the framework's approach to audio synthesis and real-time processing. Document the modular architecture, hardware requirements, and supported platforms.

wiki_catalogs.name: Buffer Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the audio buffer management subsystem. Explain the producer-consumer pattern implementation using spinlocks for thread safety, buffer pool architecture with free and prepared lists, and memory allocation strategies. Document the audio_buffer_pool_t structure, buffer lifecycle management, and the take/give operations for producer and consumer pools. Include practical examples of buffer initialization, allocation, and deallocation patterns. Explain the relationship between buffer pools and audio connections, thread synchronization mechanisms, and performance considerations for real-time audio processing. Address common buffer management issues and optimization techniques.

wiki_catalogs.name: System Design
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive system design documentation for Pico-DSP-Garden. Describe the dual-core microcontroller architecture with Core 0 dedicated to real-time audio processing and Core 1 handling sequencing/UI tasks. Document the layered architecture separating hardware abstraction, buffer management, and DSP algorithm layers. Explain the producer-consumer pattern implementation for audio buffer management and thread synchronization mechanisms. Include system initialization sequence, interrupt handling for audio callbacks, and memory management strategies. Address real-time constraints, latency requirements, and performance optimization techniques. Document the modular design principles enabling easy extension and customization of DSP algorithms.

wiki_catalogs.name: Introduction and Purpose
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the Pico-DSP-Garden introduction and purpose section. Explain the framework's core philosophy of 'Wire a DAC. Write a callback. Make sound.' and how it transforms embedded audio development for RP2350/RP2040 microcontrollers. Document the framework's mission to simplify real-time audio programming through modular design, dual-core architecture, and professional DSP integration. Include the target audience ranging from beginners exploring embedded audio to experienced developers building complex synthesizers. Explain how Pico-DSP-Garden bridges the gap between hardware constraints and creative audio synthesis. Use terminology consistent with the codebase including 'producer-consumer pattern', 'I2S DMA streaming', and 'DaisySP integration'. Provide conceptual overviews that make embedded audio accessible while highlighting the technical sophistication under the hood.

wiki_catalogs.name: Audio Engine API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for Pico-DSP-Garden audio engine interfaces. Document all audio buffer pool management functions including audio_new_producer_pool(), audio_new_consumer_pool(), take_audio_buffer(), and give_audio_buffer(). Detail audio format definitions (audio_format_t, audio_buffer_format_t), buffer structures (audio_buffer_t, audio_buffer_pool_t), and connection management APIs. Include I2S configuration functions, buffer copying operations, and audio conversion utilities. Provide complete function signatures, parameter descriptions, return values, and usage examples. Document thread safety considerations, memory management patterns, and performance characteristics for each audio API component.

wiki_catalogs.name: Oscillator Algorithms
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the oscillator algorithm family. Document the dual oscillator system including naive phasor oscillators (Sine, Triangle, Saw, Pulse) and advanced band-limited B-spline oscillators. Explain the mathematical foundations of each oscillator type, including the Phasor base class that establishes phase conventions. Detail the band-limited synthesis technique using B-spline kernels for anti-aliasing, covering the SecondOrderBSplineEventBuffer implementation and impulse scheduling. Document hard-sync and ring modulation techniques, PWM control for square waves, and noise generation. Include practical examples showing oscillator chaining, frequency modulation, and real-time parameter control. Address performance characteristics, memory usage, and optimization strategies for each oscillator type.

wiki_catalogs.name: SuperSaw Synthesizer
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the SuperSaw synthesizer example demonstrating advanced HyperSaw algorithm implementation. Explain the Hypersaw oscillator architecture with 7 oscillators, detune parameters, and waveform mixing. Document the dual-core implementation where Core 0 handles audio processing and Core 1 manages note progression and sequencing. Analyze the arpeggiator scale pattern, MIDI note conversion using mtof(), and amplitude scaling for output. Include step-by-step breakdown of the audio callback process, buffer management, and I2S configuration. Provide guidance on modifying detune values, waveform selection, and scale patterns for custom musical applications.

wiki_catalogs.name: Phasor Base Class
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the Phasor base class that establishes the fundamental phase convention used by all oscillators. Explain the phase accumulator mechanism, frequency calculation, and phase wrapping logic. Document the shared interface including prepare(), reset(), setFreq(), and process() methods. Detail the mathematical foundation of phase accumulation (phase += frequency/sampleRate) and the importance of returning phase before advancing for consistent oscillator behavior. Include practical examples showing phase initialization, frequency modulation, and phase offset techniques. Address numerical stability considerations and the wrap01() utility function.

wiki_catalogs.name: Buffer Management API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed API documentation for audio buffer management functions in Pico-DSP-Garden. Document buffer pool creation functions including audio_new_producer_pool() and audio_new_consumer_pool() with their parameters, return values, and usage patterns. Detail buffer acquisition and release functions like take_audio_buffer(), give_audio_buffer(), get_free_audio_buffer(), and queue_full_audio_buffer(). Explain buffer initialization functions including audio_new_buffer(), audio_init_buffer(), and audio_new_wrapping_buffer(). Document buffer format structures (audio_format_t, audio_buffer_format_t, audio_buffer_t) and their relationships. Include thread safety considerations, memory management patterns, and performance characteristics. Provide practical examples showing proper buffer lifecycle management and common usage scenarios.

wiki_catalogs.name: Oscillator API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for Pico-DSP-Garden oscillator classes. Document the Phasor base class with phase management, frequency control, and phase increment calculations. Detail basic waveform oscillators including SineOscillator, TriangleOscillator, SawOscillator, and PulseOscillator with their respective mathematical implementations. Document advanced band-limited oscillators including SecondOrderBSplineSawOscillator, SecondOrderBSplinePulseOscillator, and SecondOrderBSplineHardSyncSawOscillator with anti-aliasing techniques. Include NoiseOscillator for random signal generation. Provide complete method signatures, parameter ranges, initialization procedures, and practical usage examples for each oscillator type.

wiki_catalogs.name: B-Spline Kernel Theory and Implementation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the mathematical foundation of B-spline kernel techniques in anti-aliasing. Explain the concept of treating waveforms as integrals of impulses and how the 2nd-order B-spline kernel provides smooth 3-sample support for impulse smearing. Document the mathematical properties of the B-spline function, including its bell-shaped curve, C1 continuity, and superior spectral rolloff compared to linear interpolation. Detail the impulse scheduling algorithm that calculates precise sub-sample timing for discontinuities and the smear step that distributes impulse energy across neighboring samples. Include practical examples showing how the kernel reduces aliasing artifacts and maintains audio quality at higher frequencies.

wiki_catalogs.name: Getting Started
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive getting started content for Pico-DSP-Garden. Provide step-by-step installation instructions including repository cloning, development environment setup (Arduino IDE or PlatformIO), and hardware preparation. Document the complete workflow from initial setup through first successful compilation and flashing. Include detailed hardware wiring instructions for PCM510x DAC with adjustable pin configurations. Walk through opening and modifying example projects, explaining the dual-core pattern implementation with setup1() and loop1(). Provide troubleshooting guidance for common setup issues. Include verification steps to ensure proper audio output and basic parameter adjustment examples.

wiki_catalogs.name: I2S Audio Output
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the I2S audio output implementation. Document the I2S protocol configuration, PIO state machine setup for audio streaming, and DMA channel management. Explain the hardware pin mapping, clock configuration, and sample rate settings. Detail the PIO assembly code implementation for precise timing control and the integration between C++ and assembly code. Include practical examples of I2S initialization, configuration parameters, and hardware wiring requirements. Document the relationship between I2S output and buffer management, performance optimization for low-latency audio, and troubleshooting common I2S issues. Address platform-specific configurations and alternative DAC support.

wiki_catalogs.name: Component Interactions
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for component interactions within the Pico-DSP-Garden architecture. Explain the data flow from audio callback entry points through DSP algorithm processing to I2S output generation. Document the buffer pool management system connecting producers and consumers across different cores. Detail the audio connection patterns, buffer sharing mechanisms, and synchronization between audio processing threads. Include interaction diagrams showing how audio drivers communicate with DSP algorithms and how real-time constraints affect component coordination. Address error handling, buffer overflow protection, and graceful degradation strategies when real-time processing cannot keep up with demand.

wiki_catalogs.name: Hardware Requirements and Compatibility
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed content for the hardware requirements and compatibility section. Document the supported microcontrollers including Raspberry Pi Pico and Pico W (RP2040/RP2350), and the PCM510x I2S DAC requirements. Explain the default pin configuration (DATA: GPIO 15, LRCK: GPIO 16, BCLK: GPIO 17) and how to modify these settings per project needs. Include wiring diagrams and circuit requirements for basic audio output. Document hardware variations, alternative DAC options, and compatibility considerations. Provide troubleshooting guidance for common hardware issues including power requirements, signal integrity, and audio quality problems. Address platform-specific optimizations and limitations for different RP2040/RP2350 variants.

wiki_catalogs.name: DSP Algorithm API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for Pico-DSP-Garden DSP algorithm interfaces. Document oscillator class APIs including waveform generation methods, frequency control, phase management, and band-limited synthesis parameters. Detail filter algorithm interfaces covering digital filter types, coefficient calculation, state variable filtering, and parameter modulation. Include effect processor APIs for delay, chorus, flanger, reverb, and waveshaping effects. Document envelope generator interfaces for ADSR control, LFO generation, and dynamic processing algorithms. Provide complete class hierarchies, method signatures, parameter ranges, and practical usage examples for each DSP component.

wiki_catalogs.name: Filter Implementations
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the digital filter implementations. Detail the complete range of filter types including one-pole filters, biquad filters, and state-variable filters (SVF). Explain the mathematical foundations of each filter type, covering transfer functions, frequency responses, and stability considerations. Document the state-variable filter implementation with simultaneous low-pass, band-pass, and high-pass outputs. Include practical examples demonstrating filter routing, resonance control, and frequency modulation. Address parameter ranges, coefficient calculation methods, and numerical stability techniques. Document the SVF implementation with configurable Q-factor, cutoff frequency control, and mode selection.

wiki_catalogs.name: Multi-Oscillator Setup
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the multi-oscillator example demonstrating array-based oscillator management and frequency distribution. Explain how multiple oscillator instances are created and managed in arrays, frequency scaling techniques for harmonic relationships, and amplitude modulation approaches. Document the oscillator initialization process, waveform selection across multiple voices, and stereo panning strategies. Analyze the audio processing loop for handling multiple oscillator outputs, buffer management for multi-channel audio, and real-time parameter adjustment. Include examples of creating custom oscillator arrays, implementing frequency modulation between oscillators, and optimizing performance for multiple simultaneous voices.

wiki_catalogs.name: Naive Phasor Oscillators
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the four naive phasor oscillator implementations: SineOscillator, TriangleOscillator, SawOscillator, and PulseOscillator. Explain the direct mapping from phase to waveform using mathematical functions (sin, triangle, saw, square). Document the performance characteristics, memory usage, and computational efficiency of each oscillator type. Detail aliasing issues with discontinuous waveforms (saw, square, pulse) and their audible impact. Include practical examples showing frequency setting, phase reset, and real-time parameter changes. Address the simplicity of implementation and why these are considered 'naive' despite their widespread use in audio synthesis.

wiki_catalogs.name: Format Definitions API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for audio format definitions and related structures in Pico-DSP-Garden. Document audio format enumeration values (AUDIO_BUFFER_FORMAT_PCM_S16, AUDIO_BUFFER_FORMAT_PCM_S8, etc.) and their corresponding bit depths and data types. Detail the audio_format_t structure fields including sample_freq, format, and channel_count with their usage and constraints. Explain audio_buffer_format_t structure for buffer-specific format configurations. Document audio_buffer_t structure containing buffer metadata, sample counts, and user data fields. Include format conversion utilities and compatibility matrices. Provide examples of format selection for different audio quality requirements and hardware constraints. Address format validation, error handling, and best practices for format configuration.

wiki_catalogs.name: Filter API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed API documentation for Pico-DSP-Garden filter algorithms. Document one-pole filter implementations for low-pass and high-pass filtering with coefficient calculations and frequency response characteristics. Detail biquad filter structures supporting various filter types including low-pass, high-pass, band-pass, notch, and peaking EQ. Document state-variable filter (SVF) implementations with simultaneous low-pass, high-pass, band-pass, and notch outputs. Include filter parameter management, coefficient calculation methods, resonance control, and frequency modulation capabilities. Provide complete class interfaces, method signatures, parameter constraints, and practical filtering examples.

wiki_catalogs.name: SecondOrderBSplineEventBuffer Implementation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the SecondOrderBSplineEventBuffer class that handles the critical smear step in the B-spline process. Explain the 3-tap delay line architecture with its four-tap implementation for proper buffering, the impulse addition mechanism with sub-sample precision, and the sample-by-sample processing workflow. Document the addImpulse method's calculation of kernel positions based on fractional timing, the B-spline evaluation for each tap, and the sequential shifting of samples through the delay line. Include practical examples of event scheduling, timing calculations, and the relationship between event timing precision and aliasing reduction performance.

wiki_catalogs.name: Architecture Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create architectural documentation for the Pico-DSP-Garden system. Describe the high-level design featuring dual-core architecture with Core 0 handling real-time audio callbacks and Core 1 managing sequencing/UI tasks. Document the layered architecture separating audio drivers, buffer management, and DSP algorithms. Explain component interactions between the I2S audio engine, buffer pool management, and DSP algorithm modules. Include data flows from audio callback through DSP processing to I2S output. Document the modular design principles, template-based optimizations, and RAII resource management patterns. Address technical decisions around real-time constraints, memory allocation strategies, and performance optimization. Provide system context diagrams showing how audio drivers, DSP algorithms, and example projects integrate.

wiki_catalogs.name: Audio Callback System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the real-time audio callback system. Explain the dual-core architecture with Core 0 handling audio callbacks and Core 1 managing non-audio tasks. Document the audio callback registration, buffer request mechanisms, and real-time processing constraints. Detail the relationship between audio callbacks and buffer pools, latency considerations, and performance optimization techniques. Include practical examples of implementing custom audio callbacks, handling buffer overflow/underflow conditions, and integrating with DSP algorithms. Address timing requirements, interrupt handling, and best practices for real-time audio programming. Document common callback-related issues and debugging techniques.

wiki_catalogs.name: Data Flow Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed data flow architecture documentation for Pico-DSP-Garden. Trace the complete audio processing pipeline from audio callback registration through buffer allocation, DSP algorithm execution, and final I2S output transmission. Document the buffer lifecycle management including allocation, processing, queuing, and deallocation phases. Explain the sample processing workflow, format conversions, and channel routing mechanisms. Include timing diagrams showing audio callback intervals, processing latencies, and output scheduling. Address data validation, format consistency checks, and error propagation throughout the processing chain. Document performance metrics collection and monitoring points for real-time system analysis.

wiki_catalogs.name: System Architecture Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the system architecture overview section. Explain the dual-core architecture where Core 0 handles real-time audio callbacks and Core 1 manages sequencing, UI, and non-audio tasks. Document the I2S DMA streaming architecture with PIO state machines for precise timing control. Describe the modular codebase structure separating audio drivers, buffer management, DSP algorithms, and example projects. Explain the producer-consumer pattern used for audio buffer management and thread safety mechanisms. Include system flow diagrams showing data pathways from audio callback through DSP processing to I2S output. Document the integration points between hardware abstraction layers, audio engine, and DSP library. Address real-time constraints, latency considerations, and performance optimization strategies.

wiki_catalogs.name: Audio Effects
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the audio effects processing algorithms. Document the complete range of effects including delay effects, modulation effects (chorus, flanger), reverb processing, and waveshaping/distortion algorithms. Explain the mathematical principles behind each effect type, covering feedback mechanisms, modulation techniques, and convolution processes. Detail delay line implementations with variable length control, feedback attenuation, and cross-fade capabilities. Include practical examples showing effect chaining, real-time parameter modulation, and creative sound design applications. Document parameter ranges, quality settings, and performance optimization techniques for each effect type.

wiki_catalogs.name: Simple Oscillator
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the simple oscillator example demonstrating fundamental oscillator implementation with hard-sync technique. Explain the basic oscillator setup, frequency control, waveform selection, and hard-sync modulation principles. Document the minimal audio processing implementation, buffer conversion functions, and I2S audio output configuration. Analyze the hard-sync effect creation by modulating oscillator frequencies, amplitude control, and real-time parameter adjustment. Include step-by-step guidance for beginners on oscillator initialization, parameter tuning, and audio callback implementation. Provide examples of extending the basic oscillator to include envelopes, filters, and simple effects.

wiki_catalogs.name: Band-Limited B-Spline Oscillators
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the advanced band-limited oscillator family using B-spline kernel techniques. Explain the mathematical foundation of treating waveforms as integrals of impulses and the role of the 2nd-order B-spline kernel for anti-aliasing. Document the SecondOrderBSplineEventBuffer for impulse smearing across 3 samples and the leaky integrator reconstruction process. Detail the three main oscillator types: SecondOrderBSplineSawOscillator, SecondOrderBSplinePulseOscillator, and SecondOrderBSplineHardSyncSawOscillator. Include the impulse scheduling algorithm, sub-sample timing precision, and aliasing reduction techniques. Provide practical examples of oscillator chaining, hard-sync setup, and PWM control.

wiki_catalogs.name: I2S Configuration API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed API documentation for I2S audio configuration and setup functions in Pico-DSP-Garden. Document I2S initialization procedures, pin configuration requirements, and PIO state machine setup. Detail audio output configuration functions, sample rate settings, and buffer size parameters. Explain the pio_audio_channel_config_t structure for PIO-based audio channel configuration. Document DMA streaming setup, interrupt handling, and audio callback registration. Include hardware-specific configuration for different RP2040/RP2350 variants. Provide examples of complete I2S setup sequences, error handling strategies, and performance optimization techniques. Address common hardware wiring issues, timing constraints, and troubleshooting procedures for I2S audio output.

wiki_catalogs.name: Effects API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for Pico-DSP-Garden audio effects processors. Document delay effect implementations including fixed-delay lines, variable-rate delays, and ping-pong delay configurations. Detail modulation effects such as chorus, flanger, and vibrato with LFO-based modulation schemes. Document reverb processors including comb filters, allpass networks, and Schroeder reverb topologies. Include waveshaping and distortion effects with various transfer functions. Document effect parameter control, wet/dry mix blending, feedback routing, and real-time modulation capabilities. Provide complete effect class interfaces, parameter ranges, initialization procedures, and practical audio processing examples.

wiki_catalogs.name: SecondOrderBSplineSawOscillator
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the SecondOrderBSplineSawOscillator implementation as the foundational band-limited sawtooth generator. Explain the mathematical approach of reconstructing a saw waveform as the integral of wrap impulses combined with a constant negative slope, the role of the leaky integrator in DC drift prevention, and the sub-sample wrap impulse placement algorithm. Document the process method's integration of impulse smearing with slope integration, the setLeak parameter for controlling DC correction, and the relationship between frequency limits and stability. Include practical examples of oscillator setup, parameter tuning, and integration with other DSP components.

wiki_catalogs.name: Audio Engine
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the audio engine subsystem. Explain the dual-core audio architecture with Core 0 running real-time callbacks and Core 1 handling non-audio tasks. Document the buffer pool management system using producer-consumer patterns, thread safety mechanisms, and memory allocation strategies. Detail the I2S audio output implementation including PIO state machine configuration, DMA streaming setup, and hardware pin mapping. Explain the audio callback system, latency considerations, and performance optimization techniques. Include practical examples of buffer management, I2S configuration, and real-time audio processing. Document the relationship between audio drivers and higher-level DSP algorithms.

wiki_catalogs.name: Design Patterns
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Document the design patterns employed throughout the Pico-DSP-Garden architecture. Explain the implementation of producer-consumer pattern for buffer management, factory pattern for buffer pool creation, and strategy pattern for different oscillator types. Detail the use of template metaprogramming for compile-time optimizations, RAII resource management for automatic cleanup, and observer pattern for parameter updates. Include code examples demonstrating pattern implementations, their benefits for real-time audio processing, and how they contribute to system modularity and maintainability. Address pattern selection rationale, trade-offs, and extensibility considerations for future enhancements.

wiki_catalogs.name: Quick Start Guide
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the quick start guide section. Provide step-by-step instructions for setting up the development environment with Arduino IDE or PlatformIO. Document the cloning process and opening example projects from the Examples/ directory. Include detailed pin configuration adjustments and hardware wiring verification. Walk through the dual-core pattern implementation with Core 0 for audio and Core 1 for sequencing/UI tasks. Demonstrate basic DaisySP integration within audio callbacks including oscillator setup, parameter modification, and signal chain construction. Provide troubleshooting tips for common setup issues and verification steps to ensure proper audio output. Include performance optimization basics and next steps for exploring more complex projects.

wiki_catalogs.name: Envelope Generators
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for envelope generator and dynamic processing algorithms. Detail the ADSR envelope implementation with attack, decay, sustain, and release stages. Document envelope follower circuits for automatic gain control and dynamic processing. Explain the mathematical models for exponential curves, linear segments, and logarithmic scaling. Include practical examples demonstrating envelope triggering, MIDI control, and real-time modulation. Cover compressor and limiter implementations with threshold detection, compression ratio control, and lookahead processing. Address parameter automation, envelope scaling, and integration with other DSP algorithms.

wiki_catalogs.name: Two Channel Oscillator
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the two-channel oscillator example demonstrating stereo audio processing and independent channel control. Explain the dual-channel oscillator setup, independent frequency control per channel, stereo panning techniques, and phase relationship management. Document the stereo audio buffer processing, individual channel parameter control, and real-time stereo effects implementation. Analyze the audio callback for handling interleaved stereo samples, buffer management for dual-channel output, and I2S configuration for stereo audio. Include examples of creating stereo widening effects, independent modulation per channel, and implementing stereo-specific DSP algorithms.

wiki_catalogs.name: Connection Management API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for audio connection management functions in Pico-DSP-Garden. Document the audio_connection_t structure and its role in connecting producer and consumer audio pools. Detail connection establishment functions including audio_complete_connection() and default connection handlers (producer_pool_take_buffer_default, consumer_pool_give_buffer_default). Explain specialized connection types like buffer_copying_on_consumer_take_connection and producer_pool_blocking_give_connection. Document format conversion connections including mono_to_stereo_consumer_take, stereo_to_stereo_producer_give, and their variants. Include connection lifecycle management, cleanup procedures, and error handling. Provide examples of setting up different connection types for various audio routing scenarios and format conversions.

wiki_catalogs.name: Envelope API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed API documentation for Pico-DSP-Garden envelope generators and dynamic processing algorithms. Document ADSR (Attack, Decay, Sustain, Release) envelope implementations with configurable time and level parameters. Detail LFO (Low-Frequency Oscillation) generators for modulation sources with various waveform shapes and rate controls. Document envelope follower implementations for audio-reactive processing and dynamic range control. Include gate pattern generators for sequencer-style envelope triggering. Document envelope scaling, curve shaping, and external trigger handling. Provide complete interface specifications, parameter ranges, timing calculations, and practical modulation examples.

wiki_catalogs.name: SecondOrderBSplinePulseOscillator
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the SecondOrderBSplinePulseOscillator that generates band-limited square/pulse waveforms. Explain the integral-based approach using alternating edge impulses at rising and falling edges, the PWM control mechanism through phase-based edge detection, and the two-integrator system for positive and negative half-cycles. Document the addEdgeIfCrossed method for detecting phase crossings, the schedulePulseImpulses algorithm that handles both wrap and PWM edges, and the integration process that produces the final square output. Include practical examples of PWM modulation, frequency setting, and the relationship between pulse width and harmonic content.

wiki_catalogs.name: DSP Library
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the self-authored rpdsp DSP library. Explain the modular header-only design enabling easy integration and compile-time optimization. Document the complete range of DSP algorithms including oscillators (band-limited synthesis, hard-sync, ring modulation), filters (state-variable, biquad, one-pole), effects (delay, chorus, flanger, reverb), and dynamic processing (compressors, limiters). Detail the mathematical foundations, parameter ranges, and performance characteristics of each algorithm. Include practical examples demonstrating algorithm chaining, parameter modulation, and real-time control. Document the template-based design patterns, memory management strategies, and optimization techniques used throughout the library.

wiki_catalogs.name: Mathematical Foundations
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Document the mathematical and computational foundations underlying the DSP library. Explain the core algorithmic utilities including numerical precision handling, denormal number elimination, and mathematical constants. Detail the real-time programming constraints and optimization techniques used throughout the library. Document the configuration system for sample rates, buffer sizes, and performance tuning. Cover the template-based design patterns enabling compile-time optimization and code reuse. Include mathematical derivations for key algorithms, numerical stability considerations, and performance benchmarking data. Address floating-point precision, fixed-point arithmetic alternatives, and memory alignment optimizations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Algorithm Utilities API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create API documentation for Pico-DSP-Garden mathematical utility functions and audio processing helpers. Document numerical precision utilities including denormal number handling and floating-point cleanup functions. Detail mathematical constants, trigonometric functions optimized for audio processing, and logarithmic conversions. Document audio-specific utility functions including dB to linear conversion, MIDI note to frequency mapping, and sample rate validation. Include bit manipulation utilities, random number generation for noise synthesis, and mathematical convenience functions. Provide function signatures, parameter constraints, performance considerations, and usage examples for audio algorithm development.

wiki_catalogs.name: SecondOrderBSplineHardSyncSawOscillator
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the SecondOrderBSplineHardSyncSawOscillator that implements band-limited hard-sync synthesis. Explain the dual-oscillator architecture with master and slave phases, the sophisticated event scheduling that handles multiple wrap events within a single master sample, and the special impulse sizing that matches the slave's current ramp height during master resets. Document the scheduleSyncImpulses method's comparison of master and slave timing, the slave phase reset mechanism, and the integration of both wrap and sync impulses. Include practical examples of sync ratio setup, frequency relationship tuning, and the characteristic 'sync sweep' timbre generation with proper phase coordination.

wiki_catalogs.name: Example Projects
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the example projects demonstrating Pico-DSP-Garden usage patterns. Document three complete working examples: SuperSaw synthesizer with HyperSaw algorithm and dual-core sequencing, multi-oscillator setup with array-based management, and simple oscillator implementation with hard-sync technique. For each example, explain the implementation approach, key code patterns, parameter configurations, and audio processing chains. Include step-by-step analysis of how DSP algorithms are integrated, how real-time parameters are controlled, and how the dual-core architecture is utilized. Provide modification guidelines for adapting examples to create custom audio projects.

wiki_catalogs.name: API Reference
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for Pico-DSP-Garden public interfaces. Document all public functions, classes, and data structures with detailed parameter specifications, return values, and usage examples. Include audio engine APIs covering buffer pool management, I2S configuration, and audio format definitions. Document DSP algorithm APIs including oscillator class interfaces, filter method signatures, effect processor parameters, and envelope generator controls. Provide complete function signatures, parameter ranges, and typical usage patterns. Include error handling documentation, thread safety considerations, and performance implications for each API component.

wiki_catalogs.name: Configuration and Customization
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for configuration and customization options in Pico-DSP-Garden. Document build system configuration, sample rate and buffer size tuning for different performance requirements, and hardware pin mapping flexibility. Explain how to customize audio callback parameters, adjust DSP algorithm settings, and modify real-time processing chains. Include guidance on optimizing for different microcontroller variants (RP2040 vs RP2350), power consumption considerations, and thermal management. Document extension points for adding custom DSP algorithms, integrating alternative hardware, and implementing custom audio effects. Provide examples of common customization scenarios and their implementation approaches.

wiki_catalogs.name: Troubleshooting and FAQ
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive troubleshooting and FAQ content for Pico-DSP-Garden. Document common hardware issues including I2S wiring problems, DAC compatibility issues, and power supply considerations. Address audio quality problems such as noise, distortion, and dropouts with diagnostic procedures and solutions. Include performance optimization tips for reducing latency, managing CPU usage, and avoiding audio glitches. Document debugging techniques using serial output, LED indicators, and logic analyzers. Provide solutions for common software issues including compilation errors, runtime crashes, and real-time callback problems. Include frequently asked questions about hardware requirements, performance limitations, and integration challenges.

wiki_catalogs.name: Contributing and Extensions
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for contributing to and extending Pico-DSP-Garden. Document guidelines for adding new DSP algorithms including mathematical foundations, implementation patterns, and integration requirements. Explain the header-only library design principles, template usage patterns, and performance optimization techniques. Include documentation for custom hardware support, alternative DAC integration, and platform-specific optimizations. Provide contribution guidelines covering code style, testing requirements, and documentation standards. Document extension mechanisms for custom audio effects, synthesis algorithms, and hardware abstractions. Include examples of successful community contributions and integration patterns.

wiki_overview.content:
<blog>

# Pico-DSP-Garden: A Comprehensive Embedded Audio Framework for RP2350

## Project Introduction

**Pico-DSP-Garden** is an open-source C++ framework designed specifically for building audio digital signal processing (DSP) projects on Raspberry Pi Pico2 (RP2350) and Pico (RP2040) microcontrollers. This embedded audio playground provides developers with a complete toolkit for creating sophisticated synthesizers, audio effects, and real-time audio applications directly on resource-constrained hardware.

### Purpose Statement
The framework serves as a bridge between professional audio software development practices and embedded microcontroller platforms, enabling musicians, hobbyists, and developers to create high-quality audio applications with minimal complexity.

### Core Goals and Objectives
- **Real-time Performance**: Deliver deterministic audio processing with guaranteed timing constraints
- **Hardware Abstraction**: Provide clean interfaces for I2S audio output and hardware-specific optimizations
- **Modular Architecture**: Enable selective inclusion of DSP components and audio drivers
- **Dual-core Utilization**: Leverage the RP2350's dual-core architecture for optimal performance
- **Professional DSP**: Integrate industry-standard algorithms and techniques

### Target Audience
- Embedded audio developers
- Synthesizer enthusiasts
- Music technology researchers
- Educational institutions teaching audio engineering
- Makers and hobbyists interested in audio electronics

## Technical Architecture

The Pico-DSP-Garden framework follows a layered architecture that separates concerns between hardware abstraction, audio processing, and application logic.

```mermaid
graph TD
    A[Application Layer] --> B[DSP Library Layer]
    B --> C[Audio Driver Layer]
    C --> D[Hardware Interface Layer]
    
    A --> E[Example Projects]
    B --> F[Oscillators]
    B --> G[Filters]
    B --> H[Effects]
    B --> I[Envelopes]
    
    C --> J[I2S Driver]
    C --> K[Buffer Management]
    C --> L[DMA Control]
    
    D --> M[RP2350 Hardware]
    D --> N[PIO State Machines]
    D --> O[DMA Controllers]
```

### Component Breakdown

**1. Audio Engine Layer**
- Real-time audio callback execution on Core 0
- Buffer management with producer/consumer patterns
- Thread-safe audio buffer pools with spinlocks

**2. DSP Library Layer**
- Header-only DSP implementations for maximum flexibility
- Band-limited oscillators with anti-aliasing
- Professional-grade audio effects and filters
- Mathematical utilities for audio processing

**3. Hardware Abstraction Layer**
- I2S audio output via PCM510x DAC
- PIO state machines for precise timing control
- DMA-driven audio streaming
- Configurable pin assignments

### Design Patterns

The framework employs several key design patterns:

**Producer-Consumer Pattern**: Manages audio buffer lifecycle between audio processing and I2S output
**Factory Pattern**: Creates audio buffer pools with configurable parameters
**Strategy Pattern**: Allows swapping different oscillator types and DSP algorithms
**Template Metaprogramming**: Enables compile-time optimization and type safety

### System Relationships

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant Core0 as Core 0 (Audio)
    participant BufferPool as Audio Buffer Pool
    participant I2S as I2S Driver
    participant DAC as PCM510x DAC
    
    Core0->>+BufferPool: take_audio_buffer()
    Core0->>Core0: Process DSP Algorithms
    Core0->>BufferPool: give_audio_buffer()
    BufferPool->>+I2S: Audio Data Ready
    I2S->>+DAC: Stream Audio Samples
    DAC-->>-I2S: Audio Output
    I2S-->>-BufferPool: Buffer Available
```

## Key Implementation

### Main Entry Points

**Primary Framework Files:**
- `README.md` - Project documentation and getting started guide
- `audio/audio.h/cpp` - Core audio driver and buffer management
- `audio/audio_i2s.h` - I2S audio output implementation
- `dsp/oscillator.h` - Advanced oscillator implementations
- `dsp/config.h` - Framework configuration and constants

### Core Modules

**Audio Processing Pipeline:**
- `audio/audio.cpp` - Implements audio buffer pooling and synchronization
- `audio/buffer.h` - Memory buffer management with allocation strategies
- `audio/sample_conversion.h` - Audio format conversion utilities

**DSP Algorithm Library:**
- `dsp/oscillator.h` - Phase-accumulation oscillators with anti-aliasing
- `dsp/filter.h` - Digital filter implementations
- `dsp/effects.h` - Audio effect processors
- `dsp/envelope.h` - ADSR and envelope generators

**Hardware Interface:**
- `audio/audio_i2s.c` - I2S driver implementation using PIO
- `audio/audio_i2s.pio` - Assembly code for precise timing control

### Configuration Approach

**Framework Configuration:**
- `dsp/config.h` - Defines sample rate, block sizes, and mathematical constants
- `audio/audio.h` - Audio format definitions and buffer configurations
- Example-specific configurations in individual sketch files

**Hardware Pin Mapping:**
```cpp
int PICO_AUDIO_I2S_DATA_PIN = 15;
int PICO_AUDIO_I2S_CLOCK_PIN_BASE = 16; // LRCK = 16, BCLK = 17
```

### External Dependencies

**Core Dependencies:**
- **Raspberry Pi Pico SDK**: Hardware abstraction and RP2350 peripherals
- **DaisySP Library**: Professional-grade DSP algorithms and synthesis
- **µClock**: Timing and scheduling utilities
- **Mutable Instruments**: Synthesis algorithms and techniques

**Build System:**
- Arduino IDE compatible
- PlatformIO support
- CMake build configuration available

### Integration Points

**Dual-Core Architecture:**
- Core 0: Real-time audio processing callback
- Core 1: Sequencing, UI, and non-audio tasks

**Example Integration:**
```cpp
void setup() {
    // Initialize Hypersaw oscillator
    initHypersaw();
    
    // Configure audio format and buffers
    static audio_format_t audioFormat = {
        .sample_freq = 48000,
        .format = AUDIO_BUFFER_FORMAT_PCM_S16,
        .channel_count = 2
    };
    
    // Setup I2S audio output
    setupI2SAudio(&audioFormat, &i2sConfig);
}
```

### Component Relationship Diagrams

```mermaid
classDiagram
    class AudioEngine {
        +audio_buffer_pool_t* producer_pool
        +fill_audio_buffer()
        +setup_audio_callback()
    }
    
    class Hypersaw {
        +Process() float
        +SetFreq(float)
        +SetDetune(float)
        +SetMix(float)
    }
    
    class Oscillator {
        +process() float
        +setFreq(float)
        +prepare(float)
    }
    
    class Svf {
        +Process(float) float
        +SetFreq(float)
        +SetRes(float)
    }
    
    AudioEngine --> Hypersaw
    Hypersaw --> Oscillator
    Hypersaw --> Svf
```

## Key Features

### Functionality Overview

**Advanced Synthesis Capabilities:**
- HyperSaw oscillator with authentic Roland JP-8000 emulation
- Band-limited oscillators with anti-aliasing
- Multiple waveform types (sine, triangle, sawtooth, pulse, noise)
- Hard-sync and ring-modulation capabilities

**Professional DSP Effects:**
- Chorus, flanger, and delay effects
- Dynamic processing (compressors, limiters)
- Filter types (low-pass, high-pass, band-pass, notch)
- Envelope generators and LFOs

**Real-time Performance:**
- Deterministic audio callback execution
- Zero-latency buffer switching
- Optimized memory usage for embedded systems

### Implementation Highlights

**Dual-Core Audio Architecture:**
- `Examples/SuperSaw/SuperSaw.ino` - Demonstrates HyperSaw synthesis with dual-core processing
- `Examples/Oscillators/Oscillators.ino` - Multi-waveform oscillator showcase
- `Examples/SimpleOscillators/SimpleOscillators.ino` - Basic oscillator implementation

**Advanced DSP Techniques:**
- `dsp/SuperSaw.h` - Implements seven-oscillator HyperSaw with non-linear detuning
- `dsp/oscillator.h` - Band-limited B-spline synthesis for alias-free oscillators
- `dsp/algorithm.h` - Mathematical utilities for audio processing

**Hardware Optimization:**
- `audio/audio_i2s.pio` - Assembly code for precise I2S timing
- `audio/audio_i2s.c` - DMA-driven audio streaming
- `audio/buffer.c` - Efficient memory buffer management

### Feature Architecture Diagrams

```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> Initialization
    Initialization --> BufferAllocation: Setup Audio Buffers
    BufferAllocation --> AudioProcessing: Start Callback
    AudioProcessing --> I2SOutput: Fill Buffer
    I2SOutput --> AudioProcessing: Buffer Available
    AudioProcessing --> BufferAllocation: Continue Loop
    BufferAllocation --> [*]: Stop Audio
```

**Performance Characteristics:**
- Sample rates: 44.1kHz, 48kHz, 96kHz supported
- Block sizes: 16, 32, 64 samples for predictable timing
- Memory usage: Optimized for RP2350's 264KB RAM
- CPU utilization: Minimal overhead for real-time processing

**Extensibility Features:**
- Modular DSP library with header-only design
- Customizable oscillator and effect chains
- Hardware abstraction layers for different DACs
- Plugin architecture for additional audio processing

---

**Sources:**
- [README.md](README.md)
- [audio.h](audio/audio.h)
- [audio.cpp](audio/audio.cpp)
- [oscillator.h](dsp/oscillator.h)
- [SuperSaw.ino](Examples/SuperSaw/SuperSaw.ino)
- [audio_i2s.h](audio/audio_i2s.h)
- [config.h](dsp/config.h)
- [buffer.h](audio/buffer.h)
- [SuperSaw.h](Examples/SuperSaw/src/dsp/SuperSaw.h)
- [algorithm.h](dsp/algorithm.h)

</blog>
